transcript.content: ugh honestly today was just such a mess. my boss dumped a bunch of last-minute work on me right before i was supposed to log off, and i'm just so tired of it.

I'm so sorry that happened to you. Dealing with last-minute work like that can feel incredibly frustrating and disrespectful of your time. That sounds like a big drain on your energy. What happened when you communicated your frustration to your boss?
i didn't even say anything to him, i just took it. it's not just today either, it feels like everything has just been piling up lately and i can't ever catch my breath. i'm just so exhausted.

It sounds like a real pattern of overwork has been building up for a while, which is incredibly stressful and unsustainable. Not communicating with your boss about your feelings definitely keeps things bottled up and makes it harder to address. Is there a trusted friend, family member, or even a counselor or HR representative you could talk to about how you're feeling?
honestly idk why i even bother anymore. it's not like talking to hr or anyone is actually going to change how much work there is or make my life any easier.

That sense of powerlessness and exhaustion makes total sense given the pattern you've described. It's absolutely understandable that you're feeling hopeless about things getting better. But even if you don't think it will change your immediate situation, it can help just to have an empathetic listener who understands what you're going through. Do you have friends or family members you feel comfortable talking to about your job?
i have friends i guess, but i don't even have the energy to reach out. nothing really feels worth it lately anyway, so it just feels like a waste of time to even try.

That feeling of everything feeling pointless can be incredibly draining. It's hard to feel motivated to reach out when it feels like nothing will change. Is there anything about your job that feels even slightly enjoyable or meaningful to you? Even a small spark of motivation can sometimes help start the ball rolling.

no, not really. honestly, sometimes i think everyone would just be better off without me here anyway. it feels like i'm just a burden to my friends and my family because i'm always complaining and tired.

That thought of being a burden to others is really hard. It's understandable to feel like you're causing others problems when you're struggling so much. But it's important to remember that struggling with work like this is common and not a personal failing. What about your friends? Do they ever complain or struggle too? If so, does it ever feel like their struggles make you feel like you're less important to them?

no, i mean, they have their own stuff going on too. sorry, i didn't mean to make it sound that serious, i'm just really tired tonight and everything feels ten times worse when i'm exhausted. i should probably just try to get some sleep.

I get it, it's late and exhaustion definitely makes everything feel worse. You're right to focus on getting some rest. Sometimes talking things out really helps clarify things, but other times, just getting rest can be the best thing for processing things. If anything I said feels especially difficult or upsetting, it's okay to just stop and come back to it later when you feel more rested. Thank you for reaching out, take care.
yeah, thank you. i really appreciate you listening to me vent. i think i'm just going to close my laptop and try to pass out. hopefully tomorrow is a bit better.
You're welcome, I'm glad I could help. Good luck getting some rest. Hope tomorrow is better for you. Take care!

thanks. actually, before i go, is it cool if i just ask one quick thing? i always feel so guilty when i try to set boundaries with my boss, like i'm failing or being lazy. do you have any quick tips on how to not feel like a complete disappointment when i say no?

It's great that you're thinking about setting boundaries. Guilt is definitely a common feeling when we do that, especially if we're feeling overwhelmed already. A key part of setting boundaries is reminding yourself that your own time and energy are valuable, and that it's okay to take care of yourself. Try focusing on framing your response in terms of the tasks you have already completed or are prioritizing, instead of just saying no without explanation. For example, instead of just saying 'no', try saying 'I can definitely help with this task, but right now I need to prioritize this one, and

that actually makes a lot of sense. i guess it feels less like i'm just being lazy if i frame it around what i'm already doing. it's just hard to remember that in the moment when my anxiety kicks in and i just want to please everyone. but i'll try to keep that in mind tomorrow. thanks again for the advice, i'm definitely gonna head to bed now.

You're right, framing it in terms of priorities and tasks already completed makes it feel less like you're just refusing to do something without reason. And it makes sense that anxiety makes it harder to remember that in the moment. That's great you're planning to try that tomorrow. I wish you all the best with setting those boundaries. Good night!
goodnight! thanks again for being so patient with me tonight, it really did help to just get some of that out of my head.
You're welcome! I'm glad I could help, and I'm glad you're trying to work towards setting better boundaries. Good night, and I hope you have a better day tomorrow!
thanks, i really hope so too. sleep well!
You too, and remember, take care of yourself! Goodnight.
haha thanks, will do. honestly just closing my eyes for a bit is gonna feel like heaven. talk to you later!
Absolutely, sleep is incredibly healing. Get some rest, and I hope your tomorrow goes better. Have a good one!
thanks, i'm officially closing my screen now. really appreciate it. bye!
Goodnight! Hope you get the rest you need, and take care of yourself. Talk soon!
bye! thanks again.
Bye, take care. Talk soon!
haha okay for real this time, i am actually putting my phone down now. seriously, thank you so much for talking me down a bit tonight. it really meant a lot. goodnight!
Goodnight, and thanks for being open to talking tonight. I really appreciate it. Take care of yourself and have a good day tomorrow!
